[142:29]and it feels like literally everything on Twitter now

[142:33]is AI.

[142:34]Is that just me?

[143:00]I'm on Twitter and I look at a tweet

[143:03]and then I go to the replies and without fail,

[143:07]it's like every single reply on every single tweet is AI now.

[143:12]And the problem with AI is that unlike the spam bots that you used to have on Twitter,

[143:20]the AI, it almost appears organic enough that you actually have to read it to realize its

[143:27]AI.

[143:28]It's like the way that it's formatted, the way it's written.

[143:32]because if it was a spam bot,

[143:34]it would be filled with emojis

[143:36]or it would have like italicized characters and writing

[143:40]or it would be the same message,

[143:42]you know, it'd be like, you know, something in bio, whatever.

[143:46]Now the AI, they produce,

[143:49]if you just look at it without reading it,

[143:51]it just looks like a normal post, then you read it

[143:54]and you realize this is just chat GPT,

[143:57]this is AI generated.

[143:59]And it's every tweet.

[144:02]Every reply, those are all the top replies.

[144:05]And you actually have to read through like 10 or 20 replies before you get to anybody

[144:10]even real.

Topic 3: Part 1 (Trump backs down & Iran's advantage & deterrence)

[149:38]Like I said, day 24,

Topic 5: Part 3 (potential amends, weekend attacks, & the market)

[173:46]aspects of this.

[173:49]On Saturday night, President Trump, who spent the weekend at his Mar-a-Lago club, gave Iran

[173:56]an ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours, or the U.S. military would

[174:01]obliterate the country's power plants.

[174:04]Two days later, as word of the discussions in Riyadh made its way to the White House,

[174:08]Trump reversed course, embracing diplomacy with Tehran and putting his threatened strikes

[174:13]on hold.

[174:14]Trump's shift Monday morning followed a series of closed-door discussions through Middle Eastern intermediaries

[174:21]that US officials said gave them hope that an agreement to settle the conflict was possible.

[174:27]It also reflected a growing desire by Trump and some of his advisors to bring the war to a close.

[174:32]Trump's announcement that he would postpone for five days strikes on Iranian power plants triggered a stock market rally giving the president a

[174:41]small reprieve after weeks of pain on Wall Street.

[174:44]But Arab mediators privately expressed skepticism that the US and Iran could quickly reach an

[174:49]agreement, noting that the two sides remained far apart. Trump's assertion that the talks

[174:54]were productive was met with pushback from Iranian officials who denied that the discussions were

[174:58]taking place. So, like I said,

[175:41]to its support for proxy militias.

[176:10]used to manipulate the financial and oil markets,
